terms.definition: Supervised problem where the target y is a continuous numeric value and the model predicts a real number ŷ. Canonical definition in this topic.
Supervised problem where the target y is a discrete class label and the model predicts a category or class probability distribution. Canonical definition in this topic.
A numeric label y that can take values on a scale (price, temperature, score) — the hallmark of regression.
A categorical label y drawn from a finite set of classes — the hallmark of classification.
Classification with exactly two classes (e.g. positive/negative, spam/ham).
Classification where each example belongs to one of K mutually exclusive classes.
